executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period, spanning critical sectors including healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital), education (Eastern Townships School Board), public health (Peterborough Public Health), and a major consumer brand (Canada Goose hit by two separate groups). The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for a vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616) that organizations must patch immediately. High-profile victims include National Money Mart (financial services database leaked), multiple municipalities, law firms, and healthcare facilities, indicating coordinated targeting of Canadian infrastructure across all provinces.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a catastrophic ransomware wave with 101 organizations compromised across all sectors in 48 hours. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued alert AL26-007 for Fortinet FortiClientEMS vulnerability CVE-2026-35616 requiring immediate patching. Critical infrastructure targets include healthcare facilities, public health agencies, municipalities, educational institutions, and financial services with confirmed data exfiltration.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbasecartel and Shinyhunters), Active Green + Ross hit by Sinobi, Kids & Company childcare services breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware group, Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi, multiple accounting firms (MG CPA, Pewarchuk CPA) and law firms (SMT Lawyers, Bergmanis Preyra, Maheu&Maheu) breached

report_2w.executive_summary: The Canadian Cyber Centre (CCCS) issued an alert on March 30 for CVE-2026-3055, a critical vulnerability affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway products. Separately, a major supply chain compromise has emerged: 36 malicious npm packages were discovered exploiting Redis and PostgreSQL databases to deploy persistent implants, threatening software development pipelines worldwide. Ransomware groups remain active, with DragonForce claiming Innovision Holdings and Qilin linked to attacks on Japanese firms. Multiple high-severity vulnerabilities in Cisco, Keycloak, and Tenda devices require immediate patching. North Korean Lazarus Group activity and phishing campaigns targeting LinkedIn users round out the threat landscape.

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-006 on March 30, 2026, warning of CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler products. A phishing scam targeting Canadians falsely claims to be related to a milk price-fixing settlement, mirroring previous bread settlement scams. No major Canadian retailer or financial institution breaches were identified in this reporting period.
